mechanism_narrative: With FEDFUNDS pinned near the zero lower bound (0.14%), the persistently low policy rate compresses the equity discount rate and cost of capital, mechanically supporting forward S&P 500 returns as investors reach for yield in risk assets over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS=0.14 as of t-1mo, effectively at zero lower bound
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over rolling 2-year windows while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A crossing of UNRATE below the psychologically and cyclically significant 7.5% threshold signals labor market healing that raises consensus expectations of durable consumption growth, driving S&P 500 outperformance over the subsequent quarter as forward earnings estimates are revised upward.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls below 7.5% on a monthly print
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE=7.6 as of t-1mo, approaching the 7.5% threshold
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, 60-day forward returns are negative or insignificant (p > 0.05) in more than 40% of instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) jointly confirm broad-based consumer and housing demand strength that neither series alone reliably signals, and this combined demand impulse propagates into forward S&P 500 gains over the next month as cyclical sector earnings improve.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS=418284 and HOUST=917.0 as of t-1mo/t-2mo snapshot
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST rise jointly month-over-month, 20-day forward returns are positive in less than 55% of instances, or the joint-signal Rank IC does not exceed the sum of single-series IC contributions.

mechanism_narrative: A short-term acceleration in INDPRO month-over-month growth reflects tightening capacity utilization and rising industrial output, which is rapidly priced into equities within one trading week as manufacturing-sensitive S&P 500 constituents rally ahead of official earnings confirmation.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive relationship with INDPRO month-over-month acceleration (Rank IC < 0.02) over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During a zero-lower-bound regime characterized by FEDFUNDS near 0% and subdued CPIAUCSL inflation, the absence of tightening pressure allows equity valuations to expand steadily, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is contingent on the ZLB regime and reverses once inflation forces policy normalization.
regime_hint: zero lower bound / quantitative easing era
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns during ZLB-and-low-inflation episodes are not significantly higher than the full-sample unconditional average (p > 0.05), or the effect disappears once CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 2.5% without a corresponding return reversal.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of positive PAYEMS month-over-month growth and a simultaneous decline in UNRATE constitutes a confirmed labor-market strengthening signal that is more predictive than either series alone, driving forward S&P 500 gains over the next month as investors price in stronger aggregate income and consumption.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=135195 thousand, UNRATE=7.6 as of t-1mo
failure_condition: Joint PAYEMS-up/UNRATE-down episodes fail to produce 20-day forward returns exceeding the unconditional baseline by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05) over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS growth crosses above its trailing 6-month average pace, it signals an acceleration in consumer spending momentum that is quickly reflected in near-term S&P 500 price action within one trading week, as retail-exposed sectors reprice on improved revenue expectations.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth rate exceeds its trailing 6-month average growth rate
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following a trailing-average crossing are positive in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 1-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: During an early-cycle recovery regime, three consecutive months of decelerating INDPRO year-over-year growth signals a stalling industrial rebound that undermines the recovery narrative, causing equity markets to reprice growth expectations downward over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO year-over-year growth decelerates for 3 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=99.0446 as of t-2mo, GDPC1=13665.4 as of t-6mo
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following 3-month INDPRO deceleration episodes are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) relative to baseline, or the pattern reverses to positive returns in more than 50% of early-cycle triggered instances.
